segments.s477-g1: چو is the temporal subordinating particle 'when'. نامه‌بر کید هندی is a genitive noun phrase 'the letter‑bearer of the Indian plot/stratagem' (کید modifies نامه‌بر, هندی modifies کید). رسید is a simple past 3rd‑person singular verb 'arrived' that closes the adverbial clause.
segments.s477-g2: This is a independent clause whose object فرستاده پادشا is marked by the postposed را (definite object marker). بدید is an archaic/classical past form (with prefixed ب‑) meaning 'saw' in 3rd‑person singular; the subject is omitted and understood from context.
segments.s477-g3: فراوانش is فراوان + the object suffix ‑ش ('him') meaning 'him abundantly' or 'much for him'. بستود is past 'praised'. و links to the next verb; بنواختش is the past form of نواختن with the object suffix '‑ش' — here 'to play' or 'to entertain/perform for him' (classical poetic usage).
segments.s477-g4: به نیکی is an adverbial phrase 'kindly / well' (به + a noun used adverbially). بر خویش is a prepositional phrase 'at/near himself' where خوی + possessive ‑ش = 'his own/at his side'. بنشاختش is a past verb with object suffix '‑ش' from نشاختن (to set/seat/place), meaning 'he set/seated him (by his side)'.
segments.s477-g5: بدو is the prepositional/object form 'to him'. گفت here functions as the speech verb 'said' (the listed senses are generic; in context this is the past‑tense verb). شادم is an adjectival predicate 'I am glad' (first person). ز + فرمان اوی = 'because of his command' (ز = 'because of', فرمان = 'command', وِی/اوی = 'his').
segments.s477-g6: زمانی here is temporal/adverbial 'at any time / ever'. نگردم is the negative present/subjunctive of گشتن with classical b‑less form meaning 'I will not turn/change/ depart' (used here as 'I will not break/abandon'). ز پیمان اوی = 'from his covenant/pledge' (ز = 'from', پیمان = 'pledge/covenant', اوی = 'his').
segments.s477-g7: ولیکن is the adversative conjunction 'but/yet'. برین گونه is the fixed locution 'in this manner' (بر + این/ین + گونه = 'on/at this sort/manner'). ناساخته is composed of the negative prefix نا‑ + ساخته ('made/affected') and means 'not made/unnatural or unaffected' — here 'uncomposed, unbecoming'.
segments.s477-g8: بیایم is a 1st‑person subjunctive/optative form often used to express 'I will/shall come' or 'I shall come'. دمان (likely the poetic/orthographic form of دامن) literally 'skirt/hem' but here figuratively 'to his presence/side' (classical Persian uses دامن to indicate the court or someone's protection). گردن افراخته is a participial adjectival phrase 'neck raised' (گردن 'neck' + افراخته 'raised'), placed after the noun to mean 'with head/neck held high' — i.e. proudly.
segments.s477-g25: نباشد is a classical negative form of باشد used here to mean 'there is not / there will not be'; the verb comes before its (indefinite) subject for poetic inversion. کسی را is the indefinite 'anyone/no one' with the enclitic را used as an emphatic/topic marker. پس از من is the postpositional phrase 'after me' (پس = 'after', از = 'from/of', من = 'me'). به نیز (literally 'to/with likewise') functions adverbially as 'likewise/like this', modifying the whole clause.
segments.s477-g26: بدین گونه is a fixed classical phrase meaning 'in this manner/like this' (ب- is the prefix; دین here is part of the demonstrative formation and functions as part of 'بدین'). اندر جهان is the locative 'in the world' (اندر = 'in/within', جهان = 'world'). چار چیز is the classical order for 'four things' (چار = 'four', چیز = 'things').
segments.s477-g27: فرستم is first-person singular present/future 'I send / I will send' (verb first, as normal). چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; فرمان دهد is a subordinate clause where دهد is present/subjunctive 'he gives' (gives a command). پیش اوی places the action 'in his presence / before him' with اوی an enclitic pronoun referring to 'him'.
segments.s477-g28: ازان is 'from that / because of that'; تازه گردد is a light-verb construction meaning 'become fresh/renewed' (تازه 'fresh/new', گردد 'becomes' — گرد usually marks change/becoming). دل و کیش اوی means 'his heart and his faith/creed' with اوی as the possessive enclitic 'his'.
segments.s477-g29: ازان پس is a sequential adverbial 'after that/then'; چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. فرمایدم is first-person past 'I said / I addressed' (here 'I address/ask' directed to شهریار). شهریار is the addressee 'the king'. The clause sets the time/condition for the following action.
segments.s477-g30: بیایم is the present-subjunctive/intentional form 'I come / I will come' indicating the speaker's action; پرستش کنم is a compound of the noun 'پرستش' plus the light verb کنَم meaning 'to do worship / to worship' (کنم as the verbalizing auxiliary). بنده‌وار is an adverbial/qualifying word meaning 'in a servile/humble manner', modifying the entire verbal phrase.
segments.s477-g9: نباشد is a negative present/subjunctive-like form used impersonally here (‘would not be’ / ‘is not pleasing’). پسند is a noun meaning ‘approval/liking’ and with نباشد forms the predicate ‘it would not be pleasing’. جهان‌آفرین is the target of that judgment (the Creator) and follows as the logical subject/agent of the judgement.
segments.s477-g10: نه here functions as a coordinating negative (‘nor’), linking this clause to the previous one. نزدیک normally means ‘near’, but in this older/poetic context it serves as a predicate meaning ‘acceptable/near to’ or ‘in accord with’; آن پادشاه زمین is the noun phrase governed by that predicate (‘that king of the earth’). Word order places the predicate before its complement in a terse, poetic way.
segments.s477-g11: هم‌انگه is an adverbial phrase ‘at that moment’. بفرمود is an honorific past verb meaning ‘he commanded/said’. تا introduces a result/purpose clause (‘so that’), followed by شد (past of شدن, here passive/causative ‘be made/appointed’) and دبیر (the role ‘secretary/scribe’). The clause therefore says he ordered that a secretary be made.
segments.s477-g12: قلم is the noun ‘pen’ (direct object) and خواست is simple past ‘he wanted/asked for’. The following nouns/adjectives (هندی و چینی حریر) are listed items requested — ‘Indian’ and ‘Chinese’ qualify the material (here rendered as paper/silk in context). و is the coordinator ‘and’. The sequence is a straightforward verb + list of requested items.
segments.s477-g13: مران is a proper name (subject). نامه را uses را as the definite/object marker marking ‘the letter’ as topical/theme. زود is an adverb ‘quickly/soon’. پاسخ نوشت is a verb phrase ‘wrote a reply’ (پاسخ = ‘answer/reply’ is the object of نوشت). The order (subject — object/topic — adverb — object + verb) is normal in classical Persian.
segments.s477-g14: بیاراست is a past/perfective-form verb meaning ‘he adorned/arranged’. بر + سان is a fixed pattern meaning ‘upon/like’ (بر = on/upon; سان = ‘like/as’). باغ بهشت is the simile ‘a garden of paradise’. Together the clause says he adorned (it) like a paradise-garden.
segments.s477-g15: نخست is an initial adverb ‘first’. آفرین کرد is a light-verb construction: آفرین (acclamation/praise) + کرد (past ‘did/made’) = ‘he praised’ or ‘he gave praise’. بر introduces the target of the praise (‘upon/for the Creator’), and کردگار is the noun ‘Creator/God’. This is a conventional praising formula.
segments.s477-g16: خداوند is an epithet ‘the Lord/God’ placed in apposition to کردگار. پیروز is an epithet ‘victorious’ attached postpositively. و is the coordinating conjunction ‘and’. به روزگار is a prepositional phrase (‘to/for the times’ or ‘to the age’); in this poetic register it functions as a further object/recipient of the praise. Word order keeps epithets and the coordinated element compact and emphatic.
segments.s477-g17: A nominal title phrase: خداوند is the head noun (here used as 'the Lord' / God) followed by two adjectival epithets بخشنده and دادگر. In Persian the modifiers follow the noun; و simply links the two adjectives. This whole phrase functions as a compound title.
segments.s477-g18: Repeats خداوند and then lists three abstract nouns (مردی, هوش, هنر) joined by و. Here خداوند is best read as 'the Lord (who bestows/possesses)', and the three nouns are qualities (manliness/valor, intellect, skill). The structure is appositional/attributive: the Lord of X, Y and Z.
segments.s477-g19: دگر گفت is a short clause meaning 'another (one) said' or 'he said in turn' (دگر here = 'another/again', گفت = 'said'). کز is the classical fused form of 'که + ز' introducing the subordinate clause 'that/from which' (here functioning as 'that'). نامور پادشا is the noun phrase 'renowned king' that is the subject of the following clause.
segments.s477-g20: نپیچد is a classical negative verb form (ن‑ + پیچد), here third-person singular present/subjunctive/optative meaning 'does not / shall not bend/turn'; it governs the object سر مردم پارسا ('the head of a pious person/people'). سر is literal 'head', مردم 'people' (here 'person/pious man'), and پارسا 'pious/virtuous'. The clause thus says the king will not cause the pious man's head to bend.
segments.s477-g21: نشاید is an impersonal classical expression meaning 'it is not fitting' or 'one should not'. که introduces the content clause. داریم is the present first-person plural 'we have/hold' (here used with چیزی دریغ to mean 'we should have any reluctance'). چیزی is the indefinite 'anything/something', and دریغ means 'reluctance/pity' — together 'any reluctance'.
segments.s477-g22: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of/toward' marking source or reference. دارنده ('bearer' or 'possessor') governs a list of possessions: لشکر (army/host), تاج (crown/sovereignty), و تیغ (and sword). The phrase names the powerful person toward whom one should not feel reluctance.
segments.s477-g23: مرا here is the dative/topic marker 'for me' (expressing the speaker's point of view). چار چیزست is an existential/predicative phrase: چار 'four' + چیزست (چیز + است) 'there are things' — together 'there are four things'. کاندر جهان ('کاندر' = 'in which / in that / in the') + جهان 'world' is an adverbial locating those four things 'in the world'.
segments.s477-g24: This relative/explanatory clause qualifies the four things: 'کسی را نبود' is an impersonal existential/negative construction 'there was no one for whom / no one had' (کسی را = 'for anyone'), with نبود 'was not / is not'. آشکار و نهان are paired adjectives 'openly and secretly' (i.e. manifest and hidden). The whole means they are not hidden from anyone, whether openly or in secret.

sentences.s477-s1: When the Indian plot's letter‑bearer arrived, he saw the king's messenger.
sentences.s477-s2: He praised him abundantly and played for him, and, treating him kindly, set him at his side.
sentences.s477-s3: He said to him, 'I rejoice because of his command; I will not at any time depart from his pledge.'
sentences.s477-s4: But I will not come to him in that uncomposed way; I will come to his presence with my neck held high.
sentences.s477-s13: After me there will be no one like this; in the world there are four such things.
sentences.s477-s14: I will send (him) when he gives the command before him; from that his heart and faith will be renewed.
sentences.s477-s15: After that, when I address the king, I will come to perform worship, humble as a servant.
sentences.s477-s5: It would not please the Creator, nor be acceptable to that king on earth.
sentences.s477-s6: At that moment he ordered that a secretary be appointed; he requested a pen, Indian paper, and Chinese silk.
sentences.s477-s7: Maran quickly wrote the reply to the letter and adorned it like a garden of paradise.
sentences.s477-s8: First he praised the Creator, the victorious Lord, and (praised) the times.
sentences.s477-s9: The Lord, the Generous and Just; the Lord (who gives) manliness, intellect, and skill.
sentences.s477-s10: Another said that a renowned king would not bend the head of a pious man.
sentences.s477-s11: It is not fitting that we should have any reluctance toward the possessor of army, crown, and sword.
sentences.s477-s12: For me there are four things in the world that are not hidden from anyone, neither openly nor secretly.
